Name: %Chg
Description: Calculates and plots the net change, expressed as a percentage, between the current bar's price (Price input) and that price a user-specified number of bars ago, providing a quick view of percentage price swings.
Analysis & Thoughts: Simple momentum measure. Essentially same as ROC but with different naming. Good for quick visual confirmation of directional moves. Low complexity, high interpretability.

Name: %R
Description: Williams %R overbought/oversold oscillator that compares the close to the recent high-low range and flags potential reversal zones.
Analysis & Thoughts: Core oscillator - already in our library. Excellent for mean reversion strategies. Works well paired with RSI/Stochastic for confirmation. Fast response to price extremes.

Name: +DI and -DI
Description: Positive/Negative Directional Indicators from Wilder's DM.
Analysis & Thoughts: Components of ADX. These show directional movement (up/down) separately. Could be useful for refining trend direction beyond just ADX strength. Need to implement proper +DM/-DM calculation.

Name: Accum Dist - PrVol
Description: Classic Accumulation/Distribution that combines price and volume by weighting volume according to where the close lies within the bar's range, cumulated over time to show whether volume is flowing into (accumulation) or out of (distribution) a market.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic volume flow indicator. Excellent for confirming breakouts and spotting divergences. Requires volume data stream. Good addition for trend confirmation.

Name: ADX
Description: Average Directional Index: measures trend strength; not directional.
Analysis & Thoughts: Core trend strength indicator. Already in library but needs proper +DM/-DM calculation (currently simplified). ADX > 25 = strong trend, < 20 = ranging market. Critical for S2/S4.
Recommended Strategies: Trend Following (S2), Risk-Adjusted Swing (S4), Multi-Timeframe Confluence (S5)

Name: ADXR
Description: Average Directional Movement Rating: average of current ADX and a trailing ADX value (smoother than ADX).
Analysis & Thoughts: Smoothed variant of ADX. Averages current ADX with previous period's ADX to reduce noise. Good for filtering whipsaws in trend strength detection. Nice enhancement after fixing ADX.

Name: Alligator
Description: Trend-following indicator composed of three smoothed moving averages (Jaw, Teeth, Lips) displaced into the future; interwoven lines represent a resting 'alligator' (no trend) while clearly separated, aligned lines indicate a directional trend ready for buy or sell participation.
Analysis & Thoughts: Bill Williams' proprietary system. 3 EMAs: Jaw(13,8), Teeth(8,5), Lips(5,3) with forward displacement. Visual trend detection - lines tangled = no trade, aligned = trend direction. Fun to implement, good for S2.

Name: Aroon Indicator
Description: Aroon Up/Down show time since highest high/lowest low over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Excellent trend timing indicator. AroonUp = bars since 35-bar high, AroonDown = bars since 35-bar low. Values 0-100. Crosses signal trend changes. AroonUp > AroonDown = uptrend. Works well for early trend detection.

Name: Aroon Oscillator
Description: Plots the Aroon Oscillator, defined as the difference between AroonUp and AroonDown over a specified period, oscillating around zero to summarize buying vs. selling pressure.
Analysis & Thoughts: Zero-line version of Aroon. Simpler visualization than dual lines. Positive = bullish momentum, negative = bearish. Good for oscillators-based strategies. Could enhance S1/S2.

Name: ATR
Description: Average True Range over Length bars.
Analysis & Thoughts: Core volatility measure. Already in library and working correctly. Measures average price movement range including gaps. Critical for S4 (Risk-Adjusted Swing) position sizing and stop placement.

Name: Ask Size
Description: Grid based quote indicator that displays the number of shares or contracts a dealer is currently prepared to sell at the inside ask price.
Analysis & Thoughts: Level 2 order book data. Shows size at ask price - large ask size = potential resistance wall. Requires enhanced market data feed but valuable for intraday S2 confirmation.

Name: Awesome Oscillator
Description: Bill Williams momentum oscillator that plots a histogram plus color-coded buy and sell setup markers.
Analysis & Thoughts: Williams' momentum indicator (same author as %R). Simple formula: 34-period SMA - 5-period SMA. Histogram crossovers signal momentum shifts. Good complement to MACD. Easy to implement.

Name: Bollinger Bands
Description: SMA of Price over Length with upper/lower bands at NumDevsUp/Down std devs.
Analysis & Thoughts: Core volatility envelope indicator. Already in library and working correctly. Price touches upper band = overbought potential, lower band = oversold. Squeezes predict explosive moves. Critical for S3 (Volatility Breakout) and S1 (Mean Reversion).

Name: Bollinger Bandwidth
Description: Difference between Upper and Lower Bollinger Bands divided by the middle average.
Analysis & Thoughts: Measures BB squeeze/expansion. Narrow bandwidth = low volatility (squeeze), wide = high volatility. Excellent for S3 (Volatility Breakout) - detects impending explosive moves after compression.

Name: CCI
Description: Commodity Channel Index over Length bars.
Analysis & Thoughts: Already in library and working. Measures deviation from statistical mean. Overbought > +100, oversold < -100. Good for S1 (Mean Reversion) entry timing.

Name: Chaikin Money Flow
Description: Histogram of Accumulation/Distribution flow divided by total volume over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Volume-flow variant of A/D. Combines price position in range with volume over a period (typically 20-21 bars). Positive = accumulation, negative = distribution. Good for S2/S3 trend confirmation.

Name: Chaikin Oscillator
Description: Difference between two EMAs of Accumulation/Distribution.
Analysis & Thoughts: Fast version of Chaikin Money Flow. Uses EMA(3) - EMA(10) of A/D line. Oscillates around zero, good for momentum shifts. Requires volume data. Nice addition for S2.

Name: CMO (Chande Momentum Osc)
Description: 100 * (sum(up)-sum(down)) / (sum(up)+sum(down)) over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Momentum oscillator similar to RSI but simpler calculation. Range -100 to +100. Overbought > +50, oversold < -50. Good alternative to RSI for mean reversion strategies.

Name: Death Cross
Description: Detects and plots the classic death cross condition when a shorter-term moving average crosses below a longer-term moving average, often 50 vs 200 period.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic MA crossover detection (50 crosses below 200). Bearish signal. Simple to implement as dual SMA calculation with crossover detection. Good for S2 trend reversal confirmation.

Name: DeMarker
Description: Oscillator using intra-bar highs/lows; overbought/oversold levels.
Analysis & Thoughts: Oscillator similar to RSI but uses intra-bar high/close and low/close relationships. Range 0-1, overbought > 0.75, oversold < 0.25. Good diversification for mean reversion (S1) - different calculation method than RSI/Stoch.

Name: Donchian Channel
Description: Highest high / lowest low over Length; optional Displace.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic Turtle Trading system. Upper band = N-period highest high, Lower = N-period lowest low. Price breaks above/below signals trend continuation. Works with ATR for stop placement (2x ADR). Core component of S3 Volatility Breakout strategy.

Name: Donchian Width
Description: Width of Donchian Channel (Upper-Lower) over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Measures Donchian channel expansion/compression. Similar to Bollinger Bandwidth but uses N-period extremes instead of standard deviation. Narrow width = consolidation, wide = trending. Good S3 entry timing.

Name: Ease of Movement
Description: Price/volume relationship; smoothed by average.
Analysis & Thoughts: Richard's Arms 'Easemovement' formula: (High-Low) / ((Volume/100,000) / ((High+Low)/2)). Shows how easily price moves with volume. Positive = easy up movement, negative = resistance. Volume confirmation for S2.

Name: Elder Force Index
Description: Price change * volume; optional smoothing.
Analysis & Thoughts: Dr. Alexander Elder's momentum indicator: (Close - Prior Close) × Volume with 13-period SMA smoothing. Strong moves should have strong volume backing. Excellent filter for false breakouts in S2/S3.

Name: Elder Ray Index
Description: Bull Power and Bear Power vs EMA baseline.
Analysis & Thoughts: Dr. Elder's Bull/Bear Power indicator. Bull Power = High - 25-EMA, Bear Power = Low - 25-EMA. Shows strength of buyers vs sellers relative to trend line. Good for S2 trend confirmation and spotting divergences.

Name: Fibonacci Retracement
Description: Calculates and plots Fibonacci retracement high/low levels over a specified lookback, and can generate alerts when price crosses those retracement levels.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic support/resistance levels (23.6%, 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%, 78.6%) based on N-period swing high/low. Price often bounces at these levels. Good for S2 trend pullback entries and S1 mean reversion targets.

Name: Fractal (Bill Williams)
Description: Identifies price fractals (local highs/lows) per Bill Williams' method.
Analysis & Thoughts: Williams' fractal detection: 5-bar pattern where middle bar is highest high (bearish fractal) or lowest low (bullish fractal). Component of Alligator system. Good for S2 entry timing when combined with trend filters.

Name: Floor Trader Pivots
Description: Classic pivots with option to plot 5 or 7 lines.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic pivot points: Pivot = (H+L+C)/3, R1/R2 support/resistance levels above/below. Calculated from prior period's H/L/C. Price often bounces at these levels. Excellent for S2/S3 intraday entries.

Name: Gap & Reverse Dn
Description: Identifies potential bearish reversal setups where an extended up move is followed by a large gap down that fails and reverses back into the prior range; designed for intraday monitoring of gap-down reversals after multi-day price extensions.
Analysis & Thoughts: Gap reversal pattern detection. Extended up trend → gap down at open → fails to sustain lower prices and reverses back into prior range. Intraday mean reversion setup (S1) or trend failure signal (S2). Requires overnight gap analysis.

Name: Gap & Reverse Up
Description: Identifies potential bullish reversal setups where an extended down move is followed by a large gap up that fades back into the prior range; used intraday to monitor gap-up reversals after multi-day downside extensions.
Analysis & Thoughts: Gap reversal pattern detection. Extended down trend → gap up at open → fades and reverses back into prior range. Bullish mean reversion (S1) entry when gap fails to hold.

Name: Gann HiLo Activator
Description: Color-changing fast MA to identify trend direction.
Analysis & Thoughts: Simple trend filter using 3-period MA color-coded: green when price above, red when below. Fast but noisy. Could work as quick trend filter for S2 but we have better options (ADX, Aroon).

Name: Golden Cross
Description: Marks bars where a fast MA crosses above a slow MA; includes imminent-cross alerting.
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic bullish signal: 50-period MA crosses above 200-period MA. Pairs with Death Cross for complete trend detection system. Good for S2 major trend change confirmation.

Name: Heikin-Ashi
Description: Plots Heikin-Ashi candles calculated from standard OHLC.
Analysis & Thoughts: Modified candlestick calculation: HA Close = (O+H+L+C)/4, HA Open = Prior HA Open + Prior HA Close / 2. Smooths price action, reduces noise. Excellent for visual trend confirmation and filtering choppy markets. Good for S2/S3.

Name: Historical Volatility
Description: Annualized standard deviation of log returns over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Measures actual realized volatility (vs implied). Annualized std dev of daily log returns. Critical for S3 Volatility Breakout - high HV = expansive markets, low HV = compression before breakout. Also useful for option strategy comparison.

Name: HPI (Herrick Payoff Index)
Description: Herrick Payoff Index; measures money flow in futures or options markets using price, volume.
Analysis & Thoughts: John Herrick's payoff index combines price direction, volume, and open interest. Measures institutional money flow. Positive = accumulation, negative = distribution. Requires futures/options data but excellent S2 confirmation tool.

Name: Hull Moving Average
Description: WMA-based fast-responding moving average proposed by Alan Hull.
Analysis & Thoughts: Alan Hull's advanced MA: WMA(WMA(√n, 2)) - WMA(n). Extremely fast response with minimal lag. Superior to standard EMA/SMA for entry timing in S2 trend following. Could replace or complement existing MAs.

Name: Ichimoku Cloud
Description: Plots Tenkan, Kijun, Senkou Span A/B, and Chikou span to assess trend and S/R.
Analysis & Thoughts: Complete Japanese trading system. 5 components: Tenkan(9), Kijun(26), Senkou A/B (cloud projection), Chikou(span back). Price above cloud = bullish, below = bearish. Cloud breakouts signal major trend changes. Comprehensive S2 system.

Name: Kaufman Adaptive MA (KAMA)
Description: Adaptive moving average using efficiency ratio to vary smoothing.
Analysis & Thoughts: Perry Kaufman's adaptive MA adjusts sensitivity based on market noise. Uses Efficiency Ratio (net movement / total distance) to automatically smooth in choppy markets and track tightly in trends. Excellent for S2 trend following - reduces false signals.

Name: Keltner Channel
Description: EMA(Price, MaLength) with channels at Multiplier * ATR(ATRLength).
Analysis & Thoughts: Volatility channel using EMA centerline ± (Multiplier × ATR). Similar to Bollinger Bands but uses ATR instead of std dev. Less sensitive to price spikes, better for trending markets. Breakouts from Keltner often signal strong moves. Core S3 tool.

Name: Klinger Oscillator
Description: Volume-based trend oscillator with EMA signal.
Analysis & Thoughts: Stephen Klinger's volume-weighted momentum. Compares 34-period vs 55-period MA of volume flow (similar to MFI but simpler). Oscillates around zero with signal line crossover. Good for S2 trend confirmation and spotting divergences.

Name: KST (Know Sure Thing)
Description: 'Know Sure Thing' (KST) is a composite rate-of-change momentum indicator that blends four differently smoothed and weighted ROC measurements into one line with an optional signal line and zero line to track primary trend swings.
Analysis & Thoughts: Professional-grade momentum oscillator (Blair Hull). Combines 4 ROCs with different periods/weights into single smoothed line. Zero-line crossovers signal major trend changes. Signal line crossovers give earlier entries. Excellent S2 momentum confirmation.

Name: Linear Regression Curve
Description: Regression-based trend indicator that plots the end points of rolling linear regression lines through price, producing a smooth curve used to gauge trend direction and possible overextensions around the regression path.
Analysis & Thoughts: Fits linear regression line to N-period price data, plots endpoint. Smoother than any MA, shows true trend direction. Price above curve = bullish momentum, below = bearish. Good for S2 trend filtering and spotting extreme deviations.

Name: Linear Regression Slope
Description: Slope of the least-squares regression line of Price over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Regression slope shows trend angle and momentum. Positive slope = uptrend, negative = downtrend. Zero crossings signal trend changes. Good complement to regression curve for S2 confirmation - separates direction from magnitude.

Name: MACD
Description: Moving Average Convergence Divergence: EMA fast/slow difference; EMA of that difference; histogram (MACD - MACDAvg).
Analysis & Thoughts: Classic momentum/trend indicator. Already in library and working correctly. 12-26-9 default parameters. Zero-line crossovers = trend changes, signal line crossovers = entries, histogram divergences = reversals. Core S2 tool.

Name: Mass Index
Description: Volatility indicator based on range EMAs; bulge signals potential reversals.
Analysis & Thoughts: Donald Dorsey's reversal indicator. Uses double EMA of high-low range. When Mass Index rises above threshold (typically 27), then falls back below, signals potential reversal. Unique volatility compression/expansion detector. Good for S1/S3.

Name: Money Flow Index
Description: RSI-like oscillator using typical price and volume.
Analysis & Thoughts: Ginsberg's volume-weighted RSI. Typical Price = (H+L+C)/3, then positive/negative money flow ratio, smoothed to 0-100 scale. Overbought > 80, oversold < 20. Better than pure RSI for S1 because volume confirms moves.

Name: Momentum
Description: Net point change between Price and its value Length bars ago.
Analysis & Thoughts: Already in library and working. Simple price rate of change. Good for confirming trend strength and spotting divergences. Works well with S2.

Name: OBV (On Balance Volume)
Description: Cumulative volume line that adds volume on up closes and subtracts volume on down closes to gauge trend strength.
Analysis & Thoughts: Joseph Granville's classic volume indicator. Cumulative flow: +Volume when close > prior close, -Volume when close < prior close. Divergences from price signal reversals before price breaks. Excellent S2 trend confirmation and early reversal detection.

Name: Parabolic SAR
Description: Trend-following stop and reverse system with acceleration factor. Plots dots above/below price that move closer to price as trend ages, then flip when trend reverses.
Analysis & Thoughts: Wilder's stop-and-reverse system. Dots start at 0.02 acceleration, increase by 0.02 each bar until max (typically 0.2). DOTs above price = downtrend (sell stops), below = uptrend (buy stops). Excellent trailing stop for S2/S4, entry timing when dots flip.

Name: Polarized Fractal Efficiency
Description: Efficiency ratio comparing actual price travel distance vs straight-line displacement over N bars, with polarity indicating direction.
Analysis & Thoughts: Scott M. Carney's efficiency measurement. Formula: Sign(Close - Close[Length]) × (Absolute(Close - Close[Length]) / Sum(Abs(TrueRange), Length)). Range -1 to +1. Values near ±1 = efficient trending (straight line), near 0 = choppy/inefficient. Excellent for distinguishing quality trends from noise, filtering false breakouts. PFE > threshold = high-probability trend.

Name: PPO (Percentage Price Oscillator)
Description: (FastEMA - SlowEMA) / SlowEMA * 100; signal line optional. MACD expressed as percentage.
Analysis & Thoughts: MACD normalized as percentage - removes price scaling, allows direct comparison across securities with different prices. Same signals as MACD but more comparable. Good for multi-symbol scanner in S2.

Name: Price Channel
Description: Upper = highest high; Lower = lowest low over Length; optional Displace.
Analysis & Thoughts: Simple N-period high/low channel (like Donchian but no centerline). Upper breakout = bullish momentum, lower breakout = bearish. Price bouncing within channel shows relative position. Good S3 breakout detection and overextended identification.

Name: Price Envelopes
Description: Upper/Lower bands offset by Percent of a moving average.
Analysis & Thoughts: Fixed percentage channels around MA (e.g., 10% above/below 20-MA). Price touching upper envelope = overextended, lower = oversold. Unlike Bollinger Bands (adaptive), these are static percentages - better for mean reversion in ranging markets (S1).

Name: Price Volume Trend
Description: Cumulative indicator combining volume and price direction, weighting volume by percentage price change to measure trend strength via price/volume divergences.
Analysis & Thoughts: PVT formula: Prior PVT + Volume × (Current Close - Prior Close) / Prior Close. Like OBV but weights volume by percentage change rather than binary direction. Smoother than OBV, better for long-term trend confirmation. Divergences signal weakening momentum before price reversals. Good S2 confirmation tool.

Name: Pivot Range Fibonacci
Description: Pivot levels computed using Fibonacci ratios from prior period's high/low/pivot.
Analysis & Thoughts: Fibonacci-based pivot points. Standard Pivot = (H+L+C)/3, then resistance/support levels at Fibonacci ratios: R1=P+(High-Pivot)×0.382, R2=P+(High-Pivot)×0.618, R3=P+(High-Pivot)×1.0, and mirrored for S1/S2/S3. Price often bounces/reverses at these levels. Excellent for S2 pullback entries and S3 breakout targets - more granular than standard pivots.

Name: QQE
Description: Quantitative Qualitative Estimation oscillator built from smoothed RSI; double-smooths RSI-based true range to create FastTL/SlowTL trailing lines, using RSI crosses and overbought/oversold zones to assess trend and momentum.
Analysis & Thoughts: Advanced RSI variant by LazyBear. Smooths RSI(5), then creates trailing bands (FastTL=EMA(SMA(RSI),2), SlowTL=EMA(FastTL,2)). Buy when RSI crosses above FastTL in oversold zone (<38), sell when crosses below SlowTL in overbought (>61). Filters false signals better than raw RSI. Excellent S1/S2 hybrid system.

Name: RCI (Spearman's RCC)
Description: Oscillator plotting Spearman's rank correlation coefficient of price (scaled by 100) and its average, with alerts when RCI crosses its average in overbought or oversold regions.
Analysis & Thoughts: Rank-based momentum oscillator using Spearman's correlation. Measures statistical relationship between current price and N-period MA. Range -100 to +100. Crosses above/below its average in extreme zones signal reversals. Different calculation method than RSI/Stoch - good diversification for S1.

Name: Relative Strength
Description: Relative performance of current symbol vs reference symbol over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Compares two symbols' price movements (e.g., AAPL/SPY or USD/EUR). Rising line = outperformance, falling = underperformance. Useful for sector rotation, pair trading, and identifying strength within market context. Good for S2 stock selection and relative momentum strategies.

Name: Relative Vigor Index (RVI)
Description: Measures conviction of price moves by comparing close-to-open midpoint vs midprice, normalized by range to create RVI line with signal SMA.
Analysis & Thoughts: Brady's momentum oscillator. Formula: EMA((C-O)/(H-L), n) / EMA((H+L)/2, n). RVI line + signal line (SMA of RVI). Above zero = bullish conviction, below = bearish. Crossovers signal momentum shifts. Different calculation than RSI/Stoch - focuses on close vs open positioning within bar. Good S1/S2 diversification.

Name: RSI
Description: Relative Strength Index: oscillator (0-100) based on average gains vs losses over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Wilder's core momentum oscillator. Formula: 100 - (100 / (1 + RS)) where RS = Avg Gain / Avg Loss. Range 0-100, overbought >70, oversold <30. Divergences signal reversals before price breaks. Most widely used oscillator - excellent for S1 mean reversion and S2 trend confirmation.

Name: R-Squared
Description: Coefficient of determination (R²) of Price vs time over Length.
Analysis & Thoughts: Statistical trend strength measure. R² from linear regression of price over N bars. Range 0-1, higher = stronger trend (less noise). R² > 0.7 = strong trend, < 0.3 = ranging market. Pairs well with Linear Regression Slope (direction) and Curve (smoothing). Excellent for filtering choppy markets in S2.

Name: ROC
Description: Percentage rate of change over period bars.
Analysis & Thoughts: Already in library. Same as Momentum but expressed as percentage. Good for comparing momentum across different-priced securities. Useful for S2/S3.

Name: Stochastic
Description: %K and %D lines showing where price closed relative to recent high-low range.
Analysis & Thoughts: Core oscillator already in library. Overbought > 80, oversold < 20. K-D crossovers signal entries. Excellent for S1 (Mean Reversion) with RSI confirmation.

Name: Stochastic Full
Description: Full Stochastic with user-controlled slowing parameters for %K and %D smoothing periods.
Analysis & Thoughts: Complete control over Stochastic smoothing. User sets Length (lookback), Slowing K, Slowing D independently. Standard = 14/3/3, but can adjust for faster/slower response. More flexible than Fast/Slow presets - allows fine-tuning sensitivity vs noise tradeoff. Good for S1 mean reversion optimization.

Name: Swing Index
Description: Bar-by-bar swing oscillator assigning values from +100 to -100 based on current and prior bar OHLC; crosses above/below zero and extreme readings identify short-term price swings and trend direction.
Analysis & Thoughts: Weighted formula comparing close to range extremes. Values +100 (strong up) to -100 (strong down). Zero crosses signal momentum shifts. Extreme readings (>50 or <-50) show strong directional bias. Good for S2 short-term trend confirmation and overextended identification.

Name: Triangular Moving Average
Description: Double-smoothed simple moving average producing triangular weights.
Analysis & Thoughts: TMA = SMA(SMA(price, n/2), n/2). Two rounds of smoothing create triangular weighting (more weight on middle bars). Smoother than standard SMA but more lag. Can work as trend filter in S2 but we have better options (Hull, KAMA).

Name: TRIX
Description: Triple-smoothed rate of change: applies EMA three times, then calculates ROC with optional signal line.
Analysis & Thoughts: Patrick Mulloy's ultra-smooth momentum oscillator. Triple EMA eliminates noise, leaving only major trend changes. Signal line crossovers give late but reliable signals. Zero-line crossovers = major trend changes. Excellent for S2 filtering out minor fluctuations and catching big moves.

Name: True Strength Index
Description: Momentum oscillator based on double-smoothed price changes normalized between -100 and +100, with smoothed signal line to identify trend direction and potential reversals.
Analysis & Thoughts: Blair Hull's TSI (same creator as KST). Double EMA smoothing of price changes, then ratio to create -100 to +100 oscillator. Signal line crossover for entries. Very smooth - filters noise well. Good complement to KST for S2 trend confirmation.

Name: Ultimate Oscillator
Description: Weighted combination of 3 lookback periods (7, 14, 28) measuring buying pressure vs true range to reduce false signals.
Analysis & Thoughts: Larry Williams' multi-timeframe oscillator. Combines short (7), medium (14), long (28) period buying pressure measurements weighted 4:2:1. Range 0-100, overbought >70, oversold <30. Multiple timeframes reduce false signals vs single-period oscillators. Excellent S1 mean reversion with less whipsaw.

Name: Ulcer Index
Description: Measures downside risk by tracking depth and duration of price drawdowns from prior peaks; higher values indicate more stressful, volatile equity behavior.
Analysis & Thoughts: Risk measurement focusing on drawdown pain. Calculates squared drawdowns from peak, averaged over N bars, then square rooted. Higher = more painful drawdowns. Unlike standard deviation (symmetric), only measures downside risk. Excellent S4 risk filter - avoid positions with high UI.

Name: VHF (Vertical Horizontal Filter)
Description: Measures whether market is trending or ranging by comparing net price change over period to sum of absolute bar-to-bar changes.
Analysis & Thoughts: Asi's trend vs range detector. Formula: Abs(Sum(High-Low)) / Sum(Abs(Current - Prior)). Range 0-1. VHF > 0.5 = trending, < 0.5 = ranging/ranging. Excellent filter for S2/S3 - only trade breakouts when VHF confirms trend mode. Avoid mean reversion in strong trends.

Name: VWAP
Description: Volume-Weighted Average Price for the trading day; resets daily.
Analysis & Thoughts: Institutional benchmark. Cumulative (Price × Volume) / cumulative volume, resets each session. Price above VWAP = bullish intraday bias, below = bearish. Critical for S2 intraday trend confirmation and S3 breakout validation. Often acts as dynamic support/resistance. Essential for any intraday scanner.

Name: ZigZag
Description: Swing filter that draws lines between significant price swings, automatically redrawing as new extremes occur to highlight trend structure and swing points.
Analysis & Thoughts: Retrospective pattern recognition tool. Connects swing highs and lows based on percentage or point reversal threshold. NOT predictive - redraws as price moves. Useful for visualizing trend structure, counting Elliott waves, identifying support/resistance zones. Good for S2 chart analysis but not real-time scanner column.

Name: Z-Score
Description: Standard score of price relative to its moving average: measures how many standard deviations current price is from N-period MA.
Analysis & Thoughts: Statistical overextension measure. Formula: (Price - SMA) / StdDev. Range typically -3 to +3. Z-Score > +2 = significantly overbought, < -2 = oversold. Unlike Bollinger Bands (which use price), this uses statistical distance from mean. Excellent S1 mean reversion filter - identifies extreme deviations. Also useful for S3 squeeze detection.

Name: Volume Unch
Description: Grid/chart indicator that tracks daily unchanged volume — contracts or shares that traded without a price change — and plots that value (with optional alert level) so you can monitor how much trading is occurring at unchanged prices.
Analysis & Thoughts: Unchanged volume = trades at same price as prior trade. High unchanged volume = institutional block trades or market maker activity. Can indicate accumulation/distribution not visible in standard volume. S2 confirmation.

Name: Camarilla Pivots
Description: Alternative pivot point calculation with 8 support/resistance levels (S1-S4, R1-R4) using specific multipliers based on prior day's high/low/close.
Analysis & Thoughts: More granular than standard pivots - 8 levels vs typical 5-7. S1 = Close - (Range/2)*(11/10), S2=S3=S4 progressively wider, same for R1-R4. S3/R3 often act as strong intraday targets; S4/R4 breaks signal major trend days. Excellent for S2 pullback entries and S3 breakout confirmation with precise level targeting.

Name: Chande Kroll Stop
Description: Volatility-based stop-loss system using high/low bands calculated from N-period extremes minus ATR multiplier offsets.
Analysis & Thoughts: High Stop = Highest High(N) - X×ATR, Low Stop = Lowest Low(N) + Y×ATR. Unlike Parabolic SAR's accelerating dots, CKS uses fixed multipliers on channel extremes. Tighter in consolidation (highs/lows close together), widens in trends. Good trailing stop alternative for S4 risk management, different behavior than SAR.

Name: Connors RSI
Description: Advanced momentum combining three components: RSI(3) of price, rank percentile of current close vs N-period range, and length of raw up/down streak.
Analysis & Thoughts: Larry Connors' short-term mean reversion weapon. Formula: Average of (RSI(3), Percentile Rank(Close, 100), Streak RSI). Range 0-100. CRSI < 10 = extreme oversold (buy signal), > 90 = extreme overbought (sell). More responsive than standard RSI - measures momentum, trend duration, AND price position simultaneously. Excellent for S1 mean reversion on short timeframes.

Name: Fisher Transform
Description: John Ehlers' oscillator that converts price to Gaussian normal distribution, then applies inverse Fisher transform to create trigger signals at turning points.
Analysis & Thoughts: Unique math: Step 1 - Convert H/L to range -10 to +10, apply Fisher formula to get Fisher values. Step 2 - Delay Fisher by 2 bars, smooth to create Trigger line. Buy when Fisher crosses above Trigger from below, sell on reverse cross. Identifies market turning points BEFORE they happen. Completely different calculation than RSI/Stoch - no overlap in our library. Excellent S1/S2 hybrid for reversal detection.

Name: Gator Oscillator
Description: Bill Williams' histogram showing Alligator momentum - plots separation between Lips/Teeth and Jaw/Teeth as bars above/below zero.
Analysis & Thoughts: Alligator companion tool. Two histograms: Green (Lips-Teeth) above zero, Red (Jaw-Teeth) below. Bars grow = Alligator waking up (trend starting), bars shrink = Alligator sleeping (consolidation). Four growing green bars = strong uptrend, four growing red = strong downtrend. Tars/lying flat = no trade zone. Good S2 filter to confirm Alligator signals and avoid trading during 'sleeping' phases.

Name: SuperTrend
Description: ATR-based trend-following indicator plotting dynamic support/resistance bands below/above price that flip when price crosses them, showing clear BUY/SELL direction.
Analysis & Thoughts: Formula: Basic Upper Band = ((H+L)/2) + (Multiplier × ATR), Basic Lower Band = ((H+L)/2) - (Multiplier × ATR). Final bands use prior bar's band if price stays within. Green SuperTrend below price = UPTREND (buy signal when flips green), Red above price = DOWNTREND (sell when flips red). Different from Parabolic SAR: uses midpoint±ATR instead of accelerating dots, smoother flips. Very popular for swing trading and trend ID. Excellent S2 entry/exit signals with built-in trailing stop.

Name: Vortex Indicator (+VI/-VI)
Description: Measures directional movement using high-to-low distances between consecutive bars - +VI for up moves, -VI for down moves, normalized over N periods.
Analysis & Thoughts: Etienne Botes & Douglas Siepman's trend detector. +VI = Sum(Abs(Current High - Prior Low)) / Sum(ATR), -VI = Sum(Abs(Current Low - Prior High)) / Sum(ATR). Both smoothed over 14 bars. +VI > -VI = uptrend, crossover signals trend change. Unlike ADX (which measures strength without direction) or Aroon (time-based), Vortex uses actual price geometry. Provides unique perspective on directional energy. Good S2 confirmation that complements our existing ADX/Aroon/VHF suite.
